appraisal_events.evidence: « ...ne put pas s'empêcher dans sa rage... de lui crier du fond du coeur: – Trouvez-le si vous voulez, mais du moins ne nous le dites pas. »
appraisal_events.explanation: Mme Verdurin angrily rebukes Swann for praising the Guermantes, enforcing her salon's 'unité morale' and telling him to keep such views to himself.

appraisal_events.evidence: « Voilà ! s'écria Odette, voilà les grandes choses dont je lui demande de me parler... »
appraisal_events.explanation: Odette publicly frames Swann as capable of speaking about 'great things,' which locally flatters his intellect even as she notes he avoids doing so with her.

status_effects.explanation: Odette's remark publicly credits him with intellectual depth, slightly raising his local esteem.
His standing within the Verdurin circle is curtailed as he is told to conceal his outside loyalties.
